commit.message:
Fix npm global install permission error

Configure npm to use user-writable ~/.npm-global directory instead of
system /boot/system/lib/node_modules which requires root privileges.

repository.name: HaiClaude
repository.description: Native Haiku GUI launcher for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix installer to check npm prerequisite first

Reorder installation flow to verify npm (prerequisite) before checking
for Claude Code. This ensures we install dependencies in the correct order.

New flow: check npm → install npm if needed → check claude → install claude if needed

commit.message:
Standardize all status enums across vault; add Status Standards reference

000_Admin/Status Standards.md (new):
- Single source of truth for all 5 status enums: deal_status, deal_temperature,
  submission_status, meeting_status, relationship_status
- Progression diagrams, per-value definitions, dataview filter examples,
  deprecated values table, and quick reference card per enum

Templates (5 files updated):
- Startup Template: capitalize deal_temperature options (hot→Hot, warm→Warm,
  cold→Cold); add status progression validation callout
- People Professional: relationship_status default New→Prospect; inlineSelect
  options New/Warm/Strong/Cold → Prospect/Introduced/Active/Dormant/Archived;
  remove dead Institutions: field; add progression callout
- Meeting Template: default status empty→Scheduled; add inlineSelect with
  Scheduled/Completed/Follow-up Pending/Cancelled/Rescheduled; add Logistics
  block with meta-bind status input; add progression callout
- First Meeting Template: status planned→Scheduled; inlineSelect
  planned/done/cancelled → Scheduled/Completed/Follow-up Pending/Cancelled/
  Rescheduled; add progression callout
- Scout Submission Form Template: inlineSelect Draft/Ready/Submitted →
  Draft/In Progress/Submitted/Reviewed/Accepted/Rejected/Withdrawn; add
  progression callout

MOCs (3 files updated):
- People MOC: stats warm/strong→introduced/active; heat map section headers
  Strong→Active, Warm→Introduced, New→Prospect, Cold→Dormant; add Archived
  section; all WHERE clauses updated; Thesis Relevance default "New"→"Prospect"
- Meetings MOC: stats scheduled planned→Scheduled|Follow-up Pending; done
  filter uses Completed/Follow-up Pending/Cancelled; upcoming WHERE planned→
  Scheduled|Follow-up Pending; general log WHERE excludes Scheduled; Cancelled
  section updated to include Rescheduled with status column
- Scout Submissions MOC: stats ready→inProgress, submitted count broadened to
  include Reviewed/Accepted/Rejected/Withdrawn; card label Ready to Send→In
  Progress; Action Required section split into Draft and In Progress queries;
  Submitted Pipeline shows all post-submission statuses; sector map uses
  sentStatuses array for submitted count

Dashboard (ellipsis dashboard.md):
- Upcoming Meetings query: status = "planned" → Scheduled OR Follow-up Pending

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(wal-guard): narrow overly broad pattern to specific tool patterns (#11) (#13)

* fix(wal-guard): narrow overly broad pattern to specific tool patterns (#11)

The old regex blocked any command containing both 'deploy'/'push' and
'prod' anywhere in the string, causing false positives on local git
and file operations. Replaced with 8 specific patterns targeting actual
remote operations: ssh, scp, rsync, docker compose, ansible, kubectl,
helm, terraform.

Adds test suite (30 cases) covering all acceptance criteria.

Closes #11, relates to #6

commit.message:
feat(todo,config,process,cli): add progressive disclosure via reference system (#366)

* feat(todo,config,process,cli): add progressive disclosure via reference system

Adds a reference system for TODO plans to enable progressive disclosure,
integrating tokuin for token estimation and xurl for transcript reading.
Includes a redaction pipeline to strip secrets from imported transcripts.

New subcommands:
- csa todo ref list/show/add/import-transcript
- csa tokuin estimate
- csa xurl threads

Updated documentation in patterns and skills to utilize the new reference system.

Milestone: Progressive Disclosure implementation complete.

* chore(version): bump to 0.1.96

* fix(patterns): align csa todo ref add syntax with actual CLI interface

PATTERN.md and SKILL.md for mktd/mktsk were using non-existent CLI flags
(--todo-dir, --label, --source) for `csa todo ref add`. Updated to match
the actual TodoRefCommands::Add interface: positional name arg, -t timestamp,
and --content flag.

commit.message:
feat: streaming output + cross-turn Chitta persistence (333 tests)

Streaming: text_delta events yield LLM output token-by-token. Provider
invoke_stream() on MistralAdapter (OpenAI-compat), ProviderChamber
delegates with graceful fallback to non-streaming. CLI prints chunks
immediately with flush=True.

Cross-turn Chitta: Buddhi persists across turns on StewardAgent (not
recreated per-turn). Chitta.end_turn() merges reads into prior_reads,
Gandha write-without-read detection works across turn boundaries.
Chitta serialized to PersistentMemory for cross-session awareness.

Also: pyproject.toml synced to v0.10.0, pyyaml + provider optional deps.

commit.message:
feat: quality gate error UI, parent mode, before/after, growth map

UploadPage.jsx:
- Added QualityErrorCard component: structured display of per-check failures
  with XCircle icons for errors, AlertTriangle for warnings, and link to
  Filming Guide for guidance
- handleSubmit now parses structured quality gate error detail from API
  (error === 'video_quality_check_failed') and shows QualityErrorCard
  instead of generic red text
- qualityError state cleared on new file selection

ResultPage.jsx (full redesign):
- Parent Mode toggle button in navbar (pill chip, toggles green when active)
- Parent Mode banner + ParentTipCard showing parent_tip (today's ≤10min practice)
- Score rings hidden in parent mode (too technical)
- Plain summary (plain_summary) shown as teal callout in hero card for both modes
- Encouragement line shown only in player mode when plain_summary exists
- CompareCard: before/after comparison against previous session with score
  grid, delta badges (↑↓—), and "most improved" callout banner
- GrowthChart: SVG sparkline of overall score across sessions with gradient
  fill, dot markers, score labels at first/last points, trend label
- Raw motion data hidden in parent mode
- Smooth animation delays adjusted for new card order

feat: inject cross-session context for channel continuity (#498)

* feat: inject cross-session context for channel continuity

When a contractor switches between channels (e.g. Telegram to webchat),
the agent now includes recent messages from other sessions in a
"Recent Activity (other channel)" system prompt section. This gives the
assistant awareness of what was discussed on another channel without
changing session storage or routing.

Adds FileSessionStore.get_other_session_messages() and
build_cross_session_context() section builder. The session_id flows
from the pipeline context through the agent to the prompt builder.

Fixes #488

project's established test conventions.

ADMIN_API_URL is now in js/admin-config.js, loaded only by admin-facing
pages (admin.html, brewpad.html, kiosk.html, batch.html). Public visitors
on the customer-facing site no longer receive this URL in their browser.

Note: as a public GitHub Pages repo, the URL remains visible in the repo
itself. CI/CD secret injection is the path to full removal from source.

for features with code but no user-facing surface.
